---
title: "use Lulu to reduce number of erroneous ASVs"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-13"
---

install lulu package using devtools
```{r}
#library(devtools)
#install_github("tobiasgf/lulu")  
```

load packages
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lulu)
```

```{r}
## Make Lulu matchlist =========================================================
# Lulu needs a matchlist, which is basically all the ASVs blasted against
# itself.

# First create a directory to hold the blast database

# Next, make a blast-formatted reference database from your ASVs. Here we are
# using our representative sequences fasta that we exported earlier.

## Run in terminal 
## may need to activate conda environment first: conda activate cutadptenv
## (base) akcjm146-kl22:GOA2021_mifish_mb kimberly.ledger$ makeblastdb -in data/dadasnake_20241106/filtered.seqs.fasta -dbtype nucl -out data/lulu_ref/repseq_db/repseq_db

## Back in the terminal
## Then blast the OTUs against the database
## (base) akcjm146-kl22:GOA2021_mifish_mb kimberly.ledger$ blastn -db data/lulu_ref/repseq_db/repseq_db -outfmt '6 qseqid sseqid pident' -out data/lulu_ref/match_list.txt -qcov_hsp_perc 80 -perc_identity 84 -query data/dadasnake_20241106/filtered.seqs.fasta
```

read in the asv table and match list 
```{r}
asv_table <- readRDS(here("data", "dadasnake_20241106", "filtered.seqTab.RDS")) %>%
  select(!Row.names) %>%
  select(ASV, everything())

ids <- asv_table$ASV
rownames(asv_table) <- ids
asv_table <- asv_table %>%
  select(!ASV)

matchlist <- read.table(here("data", "lulu_ref", "match_list.txt"), header=FALSE,as.is=TRUE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
```

Run Lulu
```{r}
curated_result_98 <- lulu(asv_table, matchlist, minimum_ratio_type = "min", minimum_ratio = 1, minimum_match = 98, minimum_relative_cooccurence = 0.95)
```

Peek at output
```{r}
# look at the curated table
curated_result_98$curated_table

# look at the original table
curated_result_98$original_table

# number of asvs retained 
curated_result_98$curated_count

# number of asvs discarded 
curated_result_98$discarded_count

# all orginal asvs with their parent or merged assignment 
map <- curated_result_98$otu_map
```

```{r}
write.csv(curated_result_98$otu_map, here("data", "lulu_ref", "asvmap_lulu98.csv"))
```
